notes: Core Gaussian location fixed-effect formula (exactly one mu formula required); extensively tested across dozens of files but no q-series board cell exists for a pure fixed-effect (no RE) model, so tier is kept conservative per the never-claim-supported-without-a-board-cell rule. Family also supports known-variance meta-analysis via meta_V(V=V) in the mu formula (R/drmTMB.R:2719, extract_meta_known_v), outside this census's effect_type/provider axes.
Mean fixed effects (beta_mu) are the object REML marginalizes; restricted-likelihood exactness is verified against a hand-computed reference.
Default sigma ~ 1 or fitted sigma ~ x formula, log link.
Heteroscedastic sigma ~ x fixed formula is exact under REML per the code comment; a df test explicitly counts the marginalised scale fixed effects.
Board fit_status=supported for (1 | id) in mu; kept as the structured-RE arc's baseline comparator, not itself part of the structured campaign.

40-replicate Monte Carlo: mean REML SD > mean ML SD and closer to truth; not a board cell. The related sd(id) ~ x direct random-effect-scale heterogeneity formula (heteroscedastic RE variance; tests/testthat/test-gaussian-random-effect-scale.R) is ML-implemented but explicitly REJECTED under REML (R/drmTMB.R:2030-2034, spec$random_scale$mu$n_models > 0).
Fitted and tested; no q-series board cell (board's ordinary rows track mu only).
Admitted 2026-07-08 (beta_sigma marginalized). An 8-seed bias probe shows REML debiases the sigma-RE SD at n_each>=8 but underperforms ML at n_each=3 (weak identification).

Board cell covers the independent (0 + x | id) slope only; correlated (1 + x | id) and q>2 unlabelled multi-slope blocks (1 + x1 + x2 | id) are also implemented and tested (tests/testthat/test-gaussian-random-intercepts.R:722-909) but not separately boarded.
q>2 labelled blocks admitted 2026-07-08; N=60 ladder (n_each=5,10) shows REML consistently less biased than ML on every block SD.
Independent and correlated (1 + x | id) sigma slope blocks fitted.

Independent slope, matched (1 | p | id) mu-sigma block, and unlabelled correlated (1 + x | id) sigma-only block all admitted. The LABELLED sigma-only slope covariance block sigma ~ (1 + x | p | id) with no matching mu term is explicitly REJECTED (R/drmTMB.R:8080, 'Labelled residual-scale random-slope covariance blocks are not implemented yet').

Intercept-only phylo(1 | species, tree = tree); Nibi SR475 retained-denominator coverage 0.9832 (MCSE 0.0059), 4/4 misses. The default interval is the location-axis bias-corrected small-sample-t Wald direct-SD channel; this is inference-ready with caveats, not nominal or supported. The raw uncorrected log-SD Wald-z sigma channel does not apply. A weaker n=1 local smoke of the same SD target (qseries_phylo_direct_sd_univariate, mu axis) is redundant with this row.
One-slope (unlabelled, independent intercept+slope blocks) phylo(1 + x | species, tree = tree); SR475/SR150 evidence shows upper-tail, finite-interval, and hard-boundary blockers, not promoted.

Intercept-only case has an exact restricted-likelihood parity test plus a not-more-downward-biased-than-ML check. One-slope REML admission relies on the same structured_type=='phylo' gate branch but has no dedicated one-slope test found (uncertain). sd_phylo() ~ x heterogeneity formula is also REML-admitted (R/drmTMB.R:2025-2028, validated 2026-07-07).

Intercept-only sigma ~ phylo(1 | species, tree = tree); local n=5 smoke: 4/5 usable raw-Wald intervals, 1 boundary row, 1 profile failure, 3 warning replicates, not promoted. The sigma-axis direct-SD probe (qseries_phylo_direct_sd_univariate) is 0% finite at n=1, a stronger negative signal on the same target.

One-slope; g=8 deployment evidence, raw uncorrected log-SD Wald-z coverage 0.9388 (intercept SD) and 0.9935 (slope SD); intercept misses strongly upper-tail asymmetric (5 lower vs 56 upper); profile is diagnostic-only at g=8 (sigma:x profile finite rate 0.7579). The location-axis bias+t correction does not apply to sigma; this is inference-ready with caveats, not supported.

'REML admits a pure scale-side phylogenetic effect' is an admission-level test (fit + finite sdpars$sigma); the code comment claims debiasing was verified 2026-07-06 but no N-seed campaign is shown in this test file.

Mu half of the matched intercept-only 2x2 location-scale phylo block (1 | p | species) in mu and sigma jointly; local n=1 target-smoke only, mu-sigma correlation target is nonusable/boundary. This q2 denotes a within-response mu-sigma correlation block, distinct from the bivariate mu1-mu2 q2 owned by the separate biv_gaussian family.
Sigma half of the same matched intercept-only block; see paired mu row for the shared evidence and caveats.
Mu half of the matched one-slope block; native point-fit and extractor evidence for the 4 endpoint members plus 1 deterministic same-target fixture; no interval evidence.
Sigma half of the same matched one-slope block; see paired mu row.

Mu half of the matched intercept-only q2 block; the 'REML admits matched mean-and-scale phylogenetic effects' test fits n=90 (below the ~N>=250 identifiability floor the code comment cites) and asserts convergence plus non-null sdpars only, not tight recovery. A separate N=250-2000, 30-seed bias ladder is referenced in the code comment ('doc 221') but was not independently located in this pass (uncertain).

Sigma half of the same matched q2 block; see paired mu row. One-slope matched REML admission is inferred from the same structured_type=='phylo' gate branch; no dedicated one-slope-matched REML test found (uncertain).

LABELLED intercept-plus-slope structured covariance (e.g. phylo(1 + x | p | species) with a correlation label) is rejected for univariate Gaussian in both mu and sigma; the code comment states this shape is 'only implemented for the all-four bivariate Gaussian block'. Representative row: the same combine_univariate_structured_terms gate applies uniformly across all 5 providers (phylo/spatial/animal/relmat/phylo_interaction).

Intercept-only spatial(1 | site, coords = coords); coordinate-based fixed covariance only, precomputed-mesh spatial (mesh = ...) is planned but not implemented (R/drmTMB.R:8434-8440); SR475 coverage 0.9705, misses 4/10. The default interval is the location-axis bias-corrected small-sample-t Wald direct-SD channel; this is inference-ready with caveats, not nominal or supported. The raw uncorrected log-SD Wald-z sigma channel does not apply.
One-slope; SR475/SR150 blockers, not promoted.

Pure-mu univariate Gaussian exact REML with constant residual scale `sigma ~ 1` and no sigma random effect. Admits only unlabelled `spatial(1 | group)` or an independent intercept plus one numeric slope `spatial(1 + x | group)`. The Totoro campaign used spatial coordinates, `n_each=20`, and exactly `M={8,16,32}`: 2,400 paired recovery datasets (4,800 ML/REML fits) and 6,000 REML coverage fits (9,000 target profiles); every fit converged, every profile was valid/two-sided/finite, and target coverage was 0.937-0.960 (maximum MCSE 0.00768). The fitted structured SD scale `s_j` gives covariance `s_j^2 K_h`; node `i` has marginal SD `s_j sqrt(K_h[ii])`, which equals `s_j` only when that diagonal entry is one. Coverage is inference-ready with caveats, not nominal. Labelled, slope-only, multiple-slope, matched `mu+sigma`, sigma-random-effect, bivariate, non-Gaussian, estimated-range, fixed-effect-profile, untested-M, and `supported` claims remain excluded.
Intercept-only; local smoke 2/5 usable raw-Wald intervals, 3 boundary rows, 3 profile failures.
One-slope; SR1000 retained-denominator diagnostics but sigma:(Intercept) finite-Wald rate 936/1000=0.9360, below the 0.95 gate, so not promoted.

Scale-side only (sigma ~ spatial(...) with mu fixed-only); N=400-seed recovery shows REML debiases the intercept SD 400/400 (bias approx -0.009/-0.011/-0.019 across g=8/16/32); N=300-seed profile-CI coverage clears the g=8 inference_ready floor of 0.91 (0.969/0.926/0.947); finite-CI rate only 85-100% at low g. Not wired into the q-series board (no cell_id), so tier is capped below inference_ready_with_caveats per the board-linkage rule; the after-task doc itself states 'supported NOT claimed'.
Mu half of the matched fixed-covariance spatial intercept block; local n=5 smoke, finite interval rate 0.5333, pdHess 12/15, correlation target usable 0/5, blocked.
Sigma half of the same matched block; see paired mu row.
Mu half of the matched one-slope block; 4-endpoint point-fit/extractor evidence plus 1 deterministic fixture only.
Sigma half of the same matched block; see paired mu row.
Mean+scale-spanning non-phylo effect is rejected EVEN with matching (1 | p | id) labels on both sides ('only pure scale-side is validated'); representative row for intercept and one-slope shapes.
Sigma half of the same rejected matched combo.

Intercept-only animal(1 | id, A = A); SR475 473/475 usable Wald intervals, but hard-seed local replay shows endpoint profile finite 2/2 both upper misses vs truth, tmbprofile 0/2 finite; a boundary/profile-shape blocker, not an MCSE problem, not promoted.
One-slope; SR475/SR150 blockers, not promoted.

Pure-mu univariate Gaussian exact REML with constant residual scale `sigma ~ 1` and no sigma random effect. Admits only unlabelled `animal(1 | group)` or an independent intercept plus one numeric slope `animal(1 + x | group)`. The Totoro campaign used the `A` matrix from the fixed `M=8` pedigree and `n_each=20`: 800 paired recovery datasets (1,600 ML/REML fits) and 2,000 REML coverage fits (3,000 target profiles); every fit converged, every profile was valid/two-sided/finite, and target coverage was 0.933-0.952 (maximum MCSE 0.00791). `A`/`Ainv`/pedigree parity is established only by deterministic fixtures, not by the multi-seed campaign. The fitted structured SD scale `s_j` gives covariance `s_j^2 K_h`; node `i` has marginal SD `s_j sqrt(K_h[ii])`, which equals `s_j` only when that diagonal entry is one. Coverage is inference-ready with caveats, not nominal. Labelled, slope-only, multiple-slope, matched `mu+sigma`, sigma-random-effect, bivariate, non-Gaussian, other pedigrees or M, fixed-effect-profile, and `supported` claims remain excluded.
Intercept-only; SR1000 endpoint zero-boundary profile route, coverage 0.9430 (MCSE 0.007332) but upper-tail biased (lower=12, upper=45), an interval-shape blocker not an MCSE problem.

One-slope; g=8 SR1000 evidence, raw uncorrected log-SD Wald-z coverage 0.9633 (intercept SD) and 0.9895 (slope SD); misses asymmetric (intercept 26 lower vs 10 upper); profile is diagnostic-only at g=8. The location-axis bias+t correction does not apply to sigma; this is inference-ready with caveats, not supported, and pedigree/Ainv bridge marshalling remains a separate gate.

Scale-side only; N=400 recovery debiases 400/400 (bias approx -0.017 across g); N=300 coverage 0.974 at g=8, clears the 0.91 floor. Not a board cell so capped below inference_ready_with_caveats; pedigree/Ainv bridge marshalling not implied.
Mu half of the matched A-matrix intercept block; n=5 local smoke, finite interval rate 0.8000, correlation target usable 2/5, blocked.
Sigma half of the same matched block; see paired mu row.
Mu half of the matched one-slope block; 4-endpoint point-fit/extractor evidence plus 1 fixture only.
Sigma half of the same matched block; see paired mu row.
Mean+scale-spanning rejected; representative row for intercept and one-slope shapes.
Sigma half of the same rejected matched combo.

Intercept-only relmat(1 | id, K = K); K-matrix, not Q bridge marshalling; SR475 coverage 0.9789, misses 3/7. The default interval is the location-axis bias-corrected small-sample-t Wald direct-SD channel; this is inference-ready with caveats, not nominal or supported. The raw uncorrected log-SD Wald-z sigma channel does not apply.
One-slope; SR475/SR150 blockers, not promoted.

Pure-mu univariate Gaussian exact REML with constant residual scale `sigma ~ 1` and no sigma random effect. Admits only unlabelled `relmat(1 | group)` or an independent intercept plus one numeric slope `relmat(1 + x | group)`. The Totoro campaign used the `K` representation, `n_each=20`, and exactly `M={8,16,32}`: 2,400 paired recovery datasets (4,800 ML/REML fits) and 6,000 REML coverage fits (9,000 target profiles); every fit converged, every profile was valid/two-sided/finite, and target coverage was 0.932-0.960 (maximum MCSE 0.00796). `K`/`Q` parity is established only by deterministic fixtures, not by the multi-seed campaign. The fitted structured SD scale `s_j` gives covariance `s_j^2 K_h`; node `i` has marginal SD `s_j sqrt(K_h[ii])`, which equals `s_j` only when that diagonal entry is one. Coverage is inference-ready with caveats, not nominal. Labelled, slope-only, multiple-slope, matched `mu+sigma`, sigma-random-effect, bivariate, non-Gaussian, untested matrices or M, fixed-effect-profile, and `supported` claims remain excluded.
Intercept-only; SR1000 endpoint zero-boundary profile route, same blocker shape as animal sigma intercept (coverage 0.9430, upper-tail biased).

One-slope; g=8, raw uncorrected log-SD Wald-z coverage 0.9416 (intercept SD) and 0.9957 (slope SD); intercept misses upper-tail asymmetric (5 lower vs 53 upper); profile is diagnostic-only at g=8. The location-axis bias+t correction does not apply to sigma; this is inference-ready with caveats, not supported, and relmat K/Q bridge marshalling remains a separate gate.

Scale-side only; N=400 recovery debiases 400/400 (bias approx -0.020/-0.017/-0.004 across g); N=300 coverage 0.983/0.926/0.927, clears the 0.91 g=8 floor. Not a board cell so capped below inference_ready_with_caveats.
Mu half of the matched K-matrix intercept block; n=5 local smoke, finite interval rate 0.8000, blocked.
Sigma half of the same matched block; see paired mu row.
Mu half of the matched K/Q one-slope block; 4-endpoint point-fit/extractor evidence plus 1 fixture only.
Sigma half of the same matched block; see paired mu row.

Intercept-only only (coefficient shape restricted, R/drmTMB.R:8519-8525); bipartite two-tree Kronecker sparse augmented precision (R/phylo-utils.R); q1 pair-level point-fit and extractor evidence only, 'no shared phylo or row-specific interval route banked' -- no interval channel has even been attempted.

structured_mu_type() == 'phylo_interaction' != 'phylo' and the term is mu-side (not scale_side_only), so it falls into the same mean-side-non-phylo rejection branch as spatial/animal/relmat; inferred from the gate logic, no dedicated phylo_interaction REML test found (uncertain).

sigma ~ phylo_interaction(...) is never extracted (raw_structured_terms hardcodes phylo_interaction's sigma slot to NULL, R/drmTMB.R:2752); a leftover phylo_interaction() marker left in the sigma formula is instead caught by the generic drm_reject_phase1_terms structured-marker gate ('Structured-effect syntax is planned, not implemented.'). Representative row; REML is moot since ML already rejects.

q_gate in {q4,q6,q8,q12} (the all-four bivariate mu1/mu2/sigma1/sigma2 covariance engine) does not exist for this family: model_type=1 (gaussian) has no mu1/mu2/rho12 TMB data slots at all. This is a structural family boundary, not a missing feature -- q>=4 structured covariance is implemented only under the separate biv_gaussian family/model_type (family = c(gaussian(), gaussian())). Representative row standing in for all of {q4,q6,q8,q12} x all 5 providers x both estimators.

dimension=bivariate is not a mode of this family: two-response Gaussian regression is routed to the SEPARATE family/model_type biv_gaussian (family = c(gaussian(), gaussian()) or list(gaussian(), gaussian())), which has its own model_type integer and TMB data block. Representative row standing in for the entire dimension=bivariate space under family=gaussian; see a biv_gaussian census fragment for real bivariate coverage.
